A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In the final computation (execute_python:32), the agent extracted publication years by matching any 4‑digit numbers in the 'details' field and taking the earliest (min) year, which misclassified books into older decades and led to selecting "1980s" instead of the correct "2020s".
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In execute_python:32, the agent computed each decade’s average rating by averaging all review records instead of first averaging ratings per distinct book, leading it to select "2000s" rather than the correct (book-level) best decade "2020".
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In execute_python:28, the agent computed each decade’s score as the mean of per-book average ratings (equal-weighting books) instead of averaging across all individual ratings for books in that decade, causing it to return "1980s" rather than the correct "2020".
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

After execute_python:62 concluded "No decade found with 10+ distinct books" (due to not aligning purchase_id with book_id), the agent stopped and arbitrarily returned "2010s" in return_answer without using any tool-derived result.

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent pursued a reasonable plan (extract years from books_info.details and combine with review ratings) but repeatedly mis-implemented data handling in execute_python—e.g., trying to open literal file names like 'var_functions.query_db:16' instead of reading the stored path via locals()['var_functions.query_db:16'] and failing to print "__RESULT__" (see execute_python:8, :14, :38)—leading to FileNotFoundError/SyntaxError and ultimately returning a "technical difficulties" message instead of the decade.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
